nodes.title: Project tracking initialized

skill-probe
Intake orrery roadmap into beads

Design uses edges (class-level symbol usage) for orrery

uses edges cover ALL supported target languages (Python+TS/TSX/JS), not Python-only: product goal is 'visualize any repo'; implement Python as a proven vertical slice first, then extend to TS/JS within the feature
v1 uses edges are class-level only (source=class, target=class|module fallback), matching the validated prototype; function-level 'zoom in' deferred to orrery-487 (low priority)
Validate via differential test vs Python prototype on reference-repo (ground truth for Python) plus per-language fixture unit tests on tests/fixtures/repo

uses-edges is a per-plugin capability (Plugin interface gains uses-extraction: symbol-capture query + reference scan); orchestrator stays language-agnostic. Adding a language (structure+import+class+uses) requires zero core changes. Enables incremental breadth to top-10+Elixir (orrery-dom)

uses-extraction architecture = Hybrid (C): plugin provides symbolQuery + referenceQuery (declarative tree-sitter) + thin resolveSymbol(spec)->nodeId (class-preferred, module fallback, reuses resolveImport); orchestrator generically builds the per-module symbol table, scopes each captured reference to its enclosing class via parent-chain walk, applies self/own-module exclusion, dedups, and emits uses edges. Chosen over A (fully-generic engine: query over-capture risks Python differential drift) and B (fully-imperative per-plugin walk: most code per language, two paradigms in one Plugin). Key enabler: prototype filters references via the symbol table (a name not in the table = no edge), so no local-variable scope analysis is needed to hit Python parity.

uses edge target = class-preferred with module fallback (faithful to prototype): when a class references a non-class symbol (module-level function/constant/submodule), the edge targets the MODULE node. Keeps the class dependency layer complete, loses no signal, and passes the Python differential. Display clarity (telling module-layer vs class-layer vs all at a glance) is deferred to a SEPARATE renderer feature orrery-d0a, enabled because every edge endpoint already carries its node kind + parent hierarchy in the CodeMap. orrery-2uv stays scoped to extraction.
uses-edges design plan completed and committed: Architecture (Hybrid C), Existing Patterns, 5 implementation phases, 8 AC groups, Summary, Glossary. Renderer follow-up filed as orrery-d0a.
Plan implementation for uses edges (orrery-2uv)

Design renderer granularity views for uses edges (orrery-d0a)

Renderer is 3d-force-graph (ForceGraph3D/WebGL), NOT cytoscape. Already has per-edge-kind toggles (import/uses/registers) + color encoding; nodes always present; 'dim-not-remove' focus philosophy; click=drill/focus. src/render/template.html + index.ts.

Layer view = NEW top-level selector (module/class/all, default all) driving NODE visibility by dimming the off-layer (not removing), consistent with existing dim-not-remove philosophy; existing edge-kind toggles retained as finer sub-filter. Chose (a)+(c) over replacing edge toggles or hard show/hide.

Expand-module uses a DISTINCT gesture (not overloading the existing drill/focus click) and is a SEPARATE task/phase from the layer filter. 3d-force-graph has no native compound/collapsible nodes, so expand = manual graphData splice + relayout — heavier; deferred behind the filter.
DoD confirmed + design doc created: docs/design-plans/2026-07-08-render-layers.md (slug 'render-layers'). Layer filter primary + expand-module separate task.

Layer filter = dim-only suppression + minimal encoding. Reuse existing accessor+repaint machinery (dim via near-bg COLOR since nodeOpacity is global in 3d-force-graph, per research); add 'layer' state (all/module/class); extend nodeColorAcc/linkColorAcc/linkWidthAcc + label opacity + linkVisibility to fold in layer; segmented control in HUD + one-line legend caption. No graphData mutation -> stable layout, cheap, scales to large monorepos. Chose dim-only over hard-hide (keeps spatial memory, matches codebase focus philosophy) and over hybrid declutter (YAGNI).

PIVOT (supersedes node 46 dim-model): reframe as a CONTAINMENT ZOOM, not side-by-side layers. Nodes form a strict tree package>module(file)>class (a class lives in exactly one file). Default = files collapsed to a dot + import edges; expanding a file reveals its contained classes + uses edges. 'class view' = expand-all; 'expand-module' = expand-one -> SAME operation at different scope, unifying deliverables 1&2. Core mechanism: buildDisplayGraph(expanded:Set) rebuilds graphData and LIFTS every edge endpoint to its nearest visible ancestor (class in collapsed file -> the file dot), so import+uses edges stay valid at any expansion state (file->file, file->class, class->class). Uses graphData mutation (re-heats layout; pin unchanged node positions to reduce jump). Replaces the dim-only accessor approach. Motivated by user: dimming unanchored floating class dots isn't 'see files then see inside them'; containment is the right model. Better for large monorepos (collapsed default = far fewer nodes).

Design validated (containment-zoom): default collapsed files; uses lifted file->file in collapsed view; edge-lifting engine (layers.js, unit-tested + injected into template); Task1=global collapse/expand-all engine, Task2=per-file gesture + auto-expand-on-search. Ready to document.
Design complete + committed (dcab4f9): docs/design-plans/2026-07-08-render-layers.md. Containment-zoom, edge-lifting engine, 6 AC groups, 3 phases. Ready for implementation planning.
Plan implementation for render-layers (containment zoom)
Dispatched codebase investigators + 3d-force-graph research; created beads epic orrery-7ni with phase tasks orrery-318/k97/c6y

Codebase verification: all render-layers design assumptions confirmed (markers, accessors, schema, fixture: 12 classes/9 uses edges; 3d-force-graph 1.80.0 vendored). layers.js must be UMD-style (no ESM export) since template scripts are classic

Engine contract decisions: (1) links keep semantic CodeMap direction, arrow reversal stays in template shell; (2) dedup OR-merges lifted flag so class-level signal survives coincident native edges; (3) layers.js is UMD-lite (guarded module.exports + globalThis) since template scripts are classic; (4) double-click via click-timing in onNodeClick with first-click-drills (no native dblclick in 3d-force-graph); (5) persistent node-object cache + fx/fy/fz pinning for layout stability, new classes seeded at their file's coords
Implementation plan created and validated: 3 phase files + test-requirements.md, reviewer APPROVED zero issues
Execute: renderer containment zoom / layer views (render-layers)
